title: What would have happened if there was a human sized Angel
display_title: What would have happened if there was a human sized Angel
wikitext:
[]: What would have happened if there was a human-sized Angel...             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sat Dec 05, 2009 11:34 pm ...besides Kaworu? Kaworu was probably the perfect threat: he was the ultimate pilot, he had the strongest AT field, and best of all he looked human in appearance. However, he was also human in mindset; he, in the end, refused to unite with Lillith and kill us all. What if another Angel, monstrous in appearance and abilities, but human in size, had gotten into NERV HQ? Could NERV have really stopped it?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Dec 06, 2009 12:22 am I'm sure if it managed to get into Terminal Dogma, by the time it was getting ready to enter the EVAs, or at least one of them, would already be there on standby to attack the human sized angel. Gendo would probably, somehow, lure the angel in there.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Dec 06, 2009 1:47 am However, he was also human in mindset; he, in the end, refused to unite with Lillith and kill us all.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sun Dec 06, 2009 2:55 pm AuraTwilight wrote: What if another Angel, monstrous in appearance and abilities, but human in size, had gotten into NERV HQ? Could NERV have really stopped it?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Dec 06, 2009 3:14 pm Like I said previously they could wait until the angel got into Terminal Dogma. That would be the only way an Eva could attack it.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Dec 06, 2009 5:05 pm CyberXIII wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: What if another Angel, monstrous in appearance and abilities, but human in size, had gotten into NERV HQ? Could NERV have really stopped it?

[]:             Quote Post by cogitoergosum » Mon Dec 07, 2009 9:06 pm Only Hideaki Anno could answer such a question.

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Wed Dec 09, 2009 12:40 am I could see it now: Gendo: "Open the hatch to eva 00" *angel crawls in* "Initiate evangelion self destruct" *kersplodes*

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Dec 09, 2009 3:35 pm AuraTwilight wrote: No, in the end, he gave up because he realized it was Lilith, instead of Adam, as he planned. Merging with Lilith doesn't do anything for Kaworu, so he gave up in futility.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Dec 09, 2009 5:46 pm Azathoth wrote: But Kaworu knew it was Lilith, according to 24'.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Dec 09, 2009 6:14 pm But Kaworu knew it was Lilith, according to 24'. I realize the lake scene creates way more questions than it answers, but after watching it, it becomes very difficult to defend the position that Kaworu went down there expecting to find Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Dec 09, 2009 6:49 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Gendo has a part of his body. This doesn't keep Kaworu from believing the main, larger aspect of Adam's flesh is in Terminal Dogma, and he clearly thinks this is the case until he realizes what he really found.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Dec 09, 2009 8:07 pm I've heard this "part of Adam" theory before, and I still don't think the show suggests that. I have very little knowledge of Japanese, so I can't present any direct evidence here, but the subtitle on the version of the episode I'm watching is: "[Adam's] resurrected body is with Ikari." The "Kaworu's Agenda" page on the wiki has a very similar translation.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Dec 09, 2009 8:22 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Magical Angel senses. I think he can probably tell which body is his or not.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Dec 09, 2009 10:42 pm If he could tell this, why was he unable to tell that Lilith wasn't Adam in the first place? Is there a ten-foot range limit on his Giant PWM Body Identifier? Come to think of it, he specifically mentions that he's only considering causing 3I because he, like many of the other Angels, has some kind of profound, instinctual drive to return to Adam. So why is this spider-sense betraying him and drawing him towards Lilith instead?

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Thu Dec 10, 2009 6:12 pm I have a better question, what if the last angel was the size of a hamster ?
